parsed_review.summary: CP00-733 is a descriptor-only, runtime-closed RP24 Korean Legal Depth "P04 workflow permission slice" pack (range RP24.P04.M03.S21-RP24.P04.M05.S16, 40 units, risk B). Directly inspected via Read/Grep: registry.js (KOREAN_LEGAL_CP733_PACK_BINDING L196, KOREAN_LEGAL_CP733_REQUIREMENTS L1952), service.js (case-set/descriptor/Hermes-evidence/Claude-review/closeout-handoff factories L76-77,116-118 built through freezeResult + KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION), validators.js (coverage + descriptor validators L43-45), index.js (re-exports all three modules; all 6 required_public_exports present), model.test.js (CP00-733 binding/coverage L31, runtime-closed descriptor L32 asserting runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-734 and all *_runtime_opened=false, contract-projection-latest L33), README CP00-733 entry, and scripts/validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs (regenerates the contract and asserts coverage.errors==[] && descriptorValidation.errors==[] && hermes.emits_runtime_receipt==false && claude.read_only==true for cp733). Deliverable distribution {ui:18,implementation:11,claude_review:3,security_audit:4,fixture:2,test:1,hermes_evidence:1}=40 and micro-title distribution {Primary Implementation Slice:2,Secondary Workflow Slice:22,Permission And Audit Binding:16}=40 match the prompt baseline exactly; first/last unit IDs match the binding. Contract korean-legal-depth-contract.json has current_pack/latest_pack/latest_projection=CP00-733 (descriptor KoreanLegalCp733P04WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor, source_descriptor=CP732), validation.valid=true, cp733 coverage+descriptor both valid with errors:[] (L40718+), and CP722-732 preserved as historical. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, no raw/legal/credential/client payloads; permission_decision_detail_included=false; audit_event_body_included=false) and actively asserted by validateDescriptor; no fixture data files ship (fixtures are descriptor rows only). Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic control-plane review/receipt handling only, not product runtime). Review hardening satisfied: readiness-report auth preflight passed (exit_code 0, stdout_bytes 1434, valid JSON result, not 0-byte/malformed), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_review_evidence list present (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose). The manifest honestly reflects the pre-review state (status in_progress, production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only, writes_product_state=false, no enterprise-trust claim); pack_level_claude_review status=not_started with no valid receipts is the expected placeholder this run finalizes, and invalid_review_attempts is empty (not a completed-but-invalid attempt to reject). The descriptor-verified production_ready claim (flag korean_legal_p04_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified) is supported. Provenance: source was inspected directly via Read/Grep; the validation ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check), build, and tests were NOT re-executed under this read-only review (no Bash) — relied on docs/closeout-packs/cp00-733/command-evidence.json (all exit_code 0). Unrelated dirty/untracked files (workbook/*, the v2.0 docx, docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded per commit_policy.preserve_unrelated_dirty_worktree_changes and are not treated as CP00-733 scope. One P3 informational note about a benign dual-listing in the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json; no P0/P1/P2. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block this pack's closeout or the CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Pack evidence dir and handoff doc listed in both implementation and unrelated-preserved scope sets
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-733/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 175-176) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 183-184) of the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json. This is a benign labeling overlap: the pack's own evidence directory and the session handoff doc are simultaneously classified as pack implementation artifacts and as preserved dirty-worktree paths. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (corroborated by validation-report.json), so no required file was dropped from review, and the overlap has no effect on the descriptor-only deliverables, validators, contract projection, or the descriptor-verified production_ready claim. It is a scope-manifest tooling artifact, not a CP00-733 deliverable defect, and is identical to the note accepted non-blocking for CP00-732.
findings.recommendation: Optional/non-blocking: have the changed-file-scope generator de-duplicate so a path classified as an untracked implementation file is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid reader confusion.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block CP00-733 pack or goal closeout. No action required for this pack.
